candidates.row_label: Depreciation & Amortization in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: This row is an explicit COGS-related D&A line, but it appears as a component beneath/alongside the selected parent COGS total. The statement shows a total 'Cost of goods sold', then this D&A line, then another lower 'Cost of goods sold' amount consistent with an ex-D&A subtotal, indicating the selected COGS row already embeds this D&A.

The selected COGS reference bucket is the total 'Cost of goods sold' row, which appears to already include the separate D&A component shown below it. Therefore no separate COGS_DA row should be added.

variables.notes: Conservative no-add decision to avoid double counting: the explicit COGS D&A appears to be a sub-component of the already selected total COGS row, not an outside same-level expense to add separately.

candidates.why: This is an explicit SG&A-related D&A row, but it sits as a component under the selected parent 'Selling and marketing expenses' total. The amounts indicate the parent row already includes it, so it should not be added separately.

This row appears within the selected 'Other operating expenses' bucket as one component among other operating-expense items. Since 'Other operating expenses' was already selected as a parent row, adding this separately would likely double count.

variables.notes: No separate XSGA_DA should be added. The explicit SG&A-side D&A rows visible in the excerpt appear to be embedded within already selected parent expense rows ('Selling and marketing expenses' and 'Other operating expenses'). Rows labeled only 'Research and development' and 'Administration' are not explicitly D&A, so they are not selected.
